---
title: "CORA Data"
---

::: {style="text-align: justify; max-width: 860px; margin: 0 auto;"}

## ATTClimate Survey Data

The ATTClimate project has made its survey data publicly available through the **CORA** (Consorci de Serveis Universitaris de Catalunya) research data repository. CORA is the open-access repository for research data produced by Catalan universities and research institutions.

The data are deposited at [cora.csuc.cat/repositoris](https://cora.csuc.cat/repositoris/).

---

### ATTCLIMATE Survey

The **ATTCLIMATE Survey** is a four-wave panel study of the Spanish population fielded between 2023 and 2025. It tracks citizen attitudes towards climate change and support for a broad range of mitigation policies over time, enabling the analysis of opinion dynamics in response to real-world climate events and political developments.

**Coverage and sample:**

| Wave | Fieldwork period | N |
|------|-----------------|---|
| Wave 1 | February 2023 | ~4,765 |
| Wave 2 | April 2024 | ~3,019 |
| Wave 3 | October–November 2024 | ~3,734 |
| Wave 4 | 2025 | ~3,997 |

Wave 3 was split around the DANA flooding event of 30 October 2024, allowing pre/post comparisons of extreme weather event effects on climate attitudes.

Under the follow-up **ATTCLIMPOLS** project (PID2025-173985OB-I00), the panel will be extended with three further waves (2027, 2028, 2029), each with a representative sample of approximately 4,000 respondents. The resulting seven-wave dataset will span six years, making it one of the longest-running climate attitudes panels available internationally.

**Thematic coverage includes:**

- Support for climate mitigation policies (carbon taxes, low-emission zones, renewable energy, etc.)
- Beliefs about the origin and severity of climate change
- Political ideology and party preferences
- Institutional trust
- Territorial identity
- Sociodemographic characteristics

**Available materials:**

- Harmonised cross-sectional dataset (all waves stacked)
- Longitudinal panel dataset (respondents tracked across waves)
- Individual wave datasets
- Codebook (variable descriptions, value labels, availability by wave)
- Original questionnaires (Spanish and English)

**Citation:**

> Guinjoan, Marc; Rodon, Toni et al. (2026). *ATTCLIMATE: Tracking Public Attitudes and Policy Views on Climate Change*. CORA, Repositori de Dades de Recerca.

**Repository:** [cora.csuc.cat/repositoris](https://cora.csuc.cat/repositoris/)

:::

:::: funding-footer
<p class="grant-line">

<strong>ATTCLIMATE</strong> · Project TED2021-132344A-I00 · MCIN/AEI/10.13039/501100011033 · European Union "NextGenerationEU"/PRTR

</p>

<p class="grant-line">

<strong>ATTCLIMPOLS</strong> · Project PID2025-173985OB-I00 · MCIN/AEI/10.13039/501100011033

</p>

::: funding-logos
![](img/ministeri.jpg){width="220"}
:::
::::
